subject: Sociology
title: Groups and Networks
description: A short lesson to help you apply Groups and Networks.

hook.text: You got your job through a friend of a friend. Sociologists call these weak ties, and they're surprisingly powerful.

buildup.text: Social groups range from intimate pairs to massive organizations. Each type shapes behavior differently.

discovery.text: Granovetter found that weak ties — acquaintances, not close friends — are the best source of new information and jobs.

twist.text: Close friends know what you know. Acquaintances bridge different worlds and unlock hidden opportunities.

climax.text: Your network structure matters more than your network size. A few bridges outperform a thousand redundant connections.

punchline.text: The people you barely know often help you the most.

quiz.question: Why are weak ties valuable according to Granovetter?
options: They bridge different social worlds and provide new information
